body.title: [sprint] proof_emitter can't authenticate a NEW module's first test (parent-commit ImportError is collateral, not assertion-red)
body.intention: Let a ticket that introduces a brand-new module close PROVEN: give proof_emitter a way to authenticate red for a module that does not exist at the parent commit, so its first test isn't disqualified as collateral ImportError.
body.description:
**Problem (surfaced live closing T-slate-location-canonical-devlab):** proof_emitter's in-place red checks out the PARENT commit, overlays HEAD's test, and requires an authentic assertion-red (AssertionError/Failed — the stub-first convention). For a ticket that ADDS a new module, the module doesn't exist at the parent commit, so the test reds with ImportError = collateral, not authentic — the emitter fails SAFE and emits no proof. Result: genuinely-proven new-module work (green discriminating unit test + a live use-it test) cannot be closed PROVEN; it can only ship shipped-unproven. This is the missing lever that close named.

**Proposed shapes (pick on sprint):** (a) entry-point-anchored red — prove via a test that imports a module which EXISTS at the parent commit (e.g. assert the live writer's resolved path changed from old->new), so parent reds with an AssertionError; (b) stub-first-at-parent — require the new module to be committed first as a hollow stub (returns the old/wrong answer) so parent-commit red IS authentic, then the impl commit greens it; (c) teach the emitter to treat 'symbol absent at parent' as a distinct, acceptable red class for add-only tickets (with guardrails so it can't mask real collateral).

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py, devlab/claudecode/_proof_pytest_plugin.py, tests/test_proof_emitter.py. Exact set TBD on sprint.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not; log-state-changes (emit a clear classification log so 'why no proof' is visible).
**Scope boundary:** IN — make new-module work gate-provable by at least one of the shapes above, with a test proving the emitter now emits an authentic proof for an add-only ticket. OUT — removing any gate; changing the validity rules in proof_store.
**Test plan:** add a proof_emitter test that constructs a synthetic repo where HEAD adds a new module + its test, and asserts the emitter now emits a HEAD-valid proof (not a fail-safe no-op). Prove per proof-on-close.
**Completion criteria:** proof_emitter emits a HEAD-valid proof for an add-only (new-module) ticket in a synthetic-repo test (verifiable: new test green + a proof JSON written); the slate-relocation class of close can be re-proven (verifiable: emit a proof for slate_store and best_valid_proof returns it).
